title: Delissea kuhihewa
intro: Delissea kuhihewa, also known as Hawaiian hebe, is a rare and endemic plant species native to Hawaii, classified in the family Goodeniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used by Native Hawaiians to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Delissea kuhihewa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian herbalism, Delissea kuhihewa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to promote overall well-being and to treat specific health conditions.
